evals.prompt: I need to board a new retail merchant through the Payroc API. It's a clothing boutique called 'Thread & Needle' in Chicago, IL. They're an LLC, EIN 45-6789012, started the business in 2021. The owner is Maria Rossi, DOB 1979-08-14, SSN 234-56-7890, who lives at 55 Lakeview Dr, Chicago IL 60614. She's the sole owner (100%) and will be both control prong and authorized signatory. The store does about $75 average transaction, $800 peak, roughly $25k/month average. Almost all card-present (90%), rest is e-commerce. They bank at Chase — routing 021000021, account 1122334455. Use a pricing intent ID of PI-RETAIL-STD. Send the contract to sign via email. Can you build the full API request for this?
evals.expected_output: A complete, valid JSON request body for POST /v1/merchant-platforms with all required fields populated based on the provided details, plus the correct curl command or code showing the Authorization Bearer and Idempotency-Key headers. The request body should include business object with the LLC org type and correct EIN, a processingAccounts array with one entry covering all the provided details, the owner with isControlProng and isAuthorizedSignatory both true, volumeBreakdown summing to 100, amounts in cents, pricing with type:intent, and signature type:requestedViaEmail.
assertions: The response includes a complete JSON request body for POST /v1/merchant-platforms
The business object has organizationType set to privateLlc
The business taxId is 45-6789012
The owners array has isControlProng: true and isAuthorizedSignatory: true
volumeBreakdown values sum to exactly 100 (cardPresent: 90, ecommerce: 10 or similar)
transactionAmounts are expressed in cents (e.g. average: 7500 for $75)
The pricing object has type: intent and pricingIntentId: PI-RETAIL-STD
The signature object has type: requestedViaEmail
The response shows or mentions the Authorization: Bearer and Idempotency-Key headers

evals.prompt: We're integrating the Payroc boarding API and I'm getting a 400 error when I submit. Here's the error response: {"status":400,"title":"Bad request","errors":[{"parameter":"processingAccounts[0].owners","issue":"noControlProng","message":"At least one owner must be designated as the control prong"},{"parameter":"processingAccounts[0].processing.volumeBreakdown","issue":"invalidValue","message":"Volume percentages must sum to 100"},{"parameter":"processingAccounts[0].processing.transactionAmounts.average","issue":"invalidValue","message":"Value must be an integer in cents"}]. Can you explain what each of these errors means and how to fix them?
evals.expected_output: A clear explanation of all three errors with specific fixes: 1) The owners array needs at least one entry with relationship.isControlProng: true (and ideally isAuthorizedSignatory: true); 2) cardPresent + mailOrTelephone + ecommerce must add up to exactly 100; 3) transaction amounts are integers in cents, not dollars (e.g. use 5000 for $50.00). Should include corrected code snippets for each fix.
assertions: The response explains the noControlProng error and how to add isControlProng: true to an owner
The response explains that volumeBreakdown values must sum to 100
The response explains that transactionAmounts are in cents, not dollars
The response provides corrected JSON snippets or examples for each error
The response mentions that the same Idempotency-Key can be reused when resubmitting the corrected payload

evals.prompt: I want to create a merchant platform for an e-commerce client using the Payroc API. They sell software subscriptions online — SaaS company called 'CloudStack Inc', a private corporation. I don't have all the details right now. What's the minimum information I need to collect before I can make this API call? And once I have it, what does the request look like?
evals.expected_output: A clear checklist of all information required to make the API call — covering business fields (legal name, EIN, org type, address, email), processing account fields (DBA, MCC, business type, start date, timezone), owner fields (name, DOB, SSN, address, email, equity %, control prong designation), processing volumes (average and peak transaction amounts and monthly volumes, volume breakdown %), funding account (routing/account number, bank account type), and pricing info (intent ID or manual pricing). Should also mention that businessType should be 'internet' for an e-commerce SaaS company, and provide a skeleton request structure.
assertions: The response lists business information requirements (name, taxId, organizationType, address, email)
The response lists owner information requirements including SSN/national ID and control prong designation
The response lists processing volume requirements including the cents-based amounts
The response lists funding account requirements (routing number, account number)
The response mentions businessType: internet as appropriate for e-commerce
The response provides a skeleton or template of the request body structure
